summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 'Block'?

"Block" means: A group of experimental units that are similar on some characteristic expected to affect the response variable. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Randomized block design'?

"Randomized block design" means: An experimental design where units are grouped into blocks by a confounding variable, then treatments are randomly assigned within each block. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Matched pairs design'?

"Matched pairs design" means: A special case of blocking where each block has exactly two units (or one unit receiving both treatments in random order). The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Within-block variability'?

"Within-block variability" means: The variation among experimental units within the same block; reduced by effective blocking. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Randomization within blocks'?

"Randomization within blocks" means: After forming blocks, using a chance mechanism to assign treatments within each block to balance remaining confounders. The other options describe different concepts.
